titleStmt.title: SII 28.117: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 117. (A.R. No. 272 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, first prākāra, south wall.

p.hi: Śrī-Achchudaiya-mahārāya Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Mithuna śu. 14. Saturday, Anusham, Sādyayōgam, 1539 A.D. May 31.

summary.p: Records that the king installed the deity Perumāḷ, Tillai-Gōvindarājar in Perumbaṟṟa-ppuliyūr, a taniyūr in Vaḻutalampaṭṭu-usāvaḍi-mānāḍu in
1. In the bottom left hand corner. In characters of the 12th century

Vaḍakarai Irājādhirāja-vaḷanāḍu and granted as sarvvamānya, the irēkai amounting to 500 poṉ from four villages for the conduct of the worship (tiruvārādhaṉai) daily as well as on special occasions. In a subscript, the royal order also specified that five brahmins (named), would take turns in the worship. In the same place the arrangements to be made for different services including scavenging (kuppai-eḍuppāṉ) were also determined.

It is interesting that a fragment which is in the same place as the above inscription contains small portions of the praśasti commencing with Pūmālai miḍainadu of Vikrama-Chōḻa, and tells that apparently Vikramachōḻa was staying at the time in the palace at Vikramachōḻapuram. Probably he issued an order for a grant to defray the expenses on a festival in the month, Māśi in some temple in Rājarāja-ppāṇḍi-nāḍu. Even from this fragment it can be deduced that Vikramachōḻa built a capital for his own rule and named it after his own name. (Cf. Sastri KAN, The Colās, p. 332)

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svasti . śrīman mahāmaṇḍaleśvaran hariyarāya vibhāṭan bhāṣaikkut tappuvarāyara kaṇḍan
muvvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa nāṭu koṭātāṉ pūṟuva dakṣiṇa paśvima Uttara catussamutrātipati śrīvirapradāpan Irācātirācan rācaparameśvaran
śrīaścudaiya mahārāyan pridivirājyam paṇṇi Aruḷā niṉṟa śagābdamRead śakābdam 1000 6 100 10 1 Itaṉ meṟ cellāniṉṟa vikāri saṁratsarattuRead samvatsarattu mituna nāyaṟṟu
purvva pakṣattu śaturddaśiyum sādya yogamum man=tavāramum peṟṟa Anuṣa nakṣatrattu nāḷ vaṭakarai Irācātirāca vaḷanāṭṭu veṇṇaimā
nāṭṭu vaḻutalampaṭṭu Ucāvaṭi taniyūr perumpaṟṟappuliyūril svāmi Accudaiyamahārāyar perumāḷ tillaik kovintarācarai pratiṭṭai
paṇṇuvittu tiruvārātaṉaik kaṭṭaḷaikku kāverikku vaṭakarai kaṭalaṅkuṭi mākaṇaiyil cetta kaṭalaṅkuṭi cantai vaḻiccāri paṭṭaṭai
calatattā [2�Probably stands for paṭṭi] kiṟāmam 1 kulatipakanallūr kiṟāmam 1 Añcaṟai vāyttalai kiṟāmam 1 muḷḷikkuṭi kiṟāmam 1 Āka kiṟāmam nāṉku Ellaikku 1kāṇiyumProbably stands for uḷpaṭṭa
nañceycey written below the line.puñcey nattam ceṭittalaittiṭar va Probably stands for uḷpaṭṭaIrekai poṉ 5 100 Intac cirmai kiṟāmam nāliluṇṭāṉa poṉ Aññūṟum perumāḷ tillaik
kovintarācaṟku tiruvārātiṉaikku niṟṟappaṭi Eṟupaṭi Uṭpaṭa viṭṭapaṭiyā�written below the line.le māṉiyamāka cantiṟātittavaraiyum Aṉupavittuk koḷḷak kaṭavatākavum Inta taṉmat
tukku yātoruttar Ahitam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ gaṅkaiyile kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile pokakkaṭavarkaḷ Ākavum §
śvāmi Accutaiya makārāyar Uttirāpaṭṭikku tillaik kovinta Irācarai vaikāṉasa śāstrattile pratiṭṭai paṇṇi tiruvārātaṉam paṇṇak kaṭṭaḷai Iṭṭa
nampimār muppatu vaṭṭattil piṟivu tiruvatiyil kācipa kottiṟattu kuppaiyaṟku nāḷ Eṭṭu tiṇṭivaṉam pārattuvāja kottiṟattu nallaperumāḷ nāḷ Eṭṭu tiruvayintiṟapuram kā kotti
ṟattu teyvanāyaka paṭṭar nāḷ 8 tāṭaḷaṉ koyil kevutama kottiṟa ciṉivācapaṭṭaṟku nāḷ 3 muṉṟu meṟpaṭiyūr bhāradvā[jako]ttiṟattu narasimha di trināḷ muṉṟu Ākappo
Añcukku nāḷ muppatu Itu kuṭi niṟuvākak kaṭṭaḷai nampimār tāṉattār Uṭpaṭa niṟuvākam nālu yār niṟuvākam Oṉṟu poṟ paṇṭāram timmaiyar
niṟuvākam Oṉṟu koyil mutal tāṉa mutal tecāntira mutal samprati muṉṟu cīpātam tāṅkum pirāmaṇar Ūḻiyam kergyai gotrattu kuñcappūr cīrāmayar
cittiṟakūṭam tillaikkovintarācar laṭimai kaikkoḷakkāṇi tirumeṉataravalattu va Ūḻi Eḻuperu
yarai viṭavum tirunāḷkaḷukkut tiruvītiyil Eḻuntaruḷa paṇṇavum maṟṟum tirukkoyilukku veṇṭiṉa yam nampimār mutal
kuppai Eṭuppāṉ kaṭai kaṭai kaṭṭaḷai Iṭṭa pattirappaṭi Ūḻiyamuṇṭāṉatellām Avarkaḷ kāṇiyāṭci Ākakkoṇṭu Anta Antappaṭiye cantāṉa
paramparai Āka Ūḻiyam paṇṇi viṉiyokaṅkaḷ Uṇṭāṉatu peṟṟuvarakkaṭavarkaḷākavumOn the right bottom corner there is a fragment of an inscription of the reign of of Vikrama-chōḻa reading as follows
i. •••• puṇarn=ta kaṟpa[pi]naḷāki viruppoṭu • nāvakattiru
ii. [ṉa]var cumantu tiṟai niraittuc corinta cemp[o]
iii. ttuttiruvaḷar matiyi[ṉ] tiraiyotacippakkattu i .
iv. kirama coḻapurattuk koyiliṉ uḷḷāl akaḷaṅ
v. ṅki īmaṅkaḷukkum tirumācit tirunāḷaikkum
vi. ṟṟukku kūṟu rājarājappāṇṭināṭṭu rāje
Digital edition of SII 28.117 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
